preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, with Panama's win probability at 12%. The underdog's chance remains well below one-in-ten, establishing a clear hierarchical gap between the two sides in the forecast.

The 24.8% draw probability suggests moderate competitive balance despite England's advantage. A draw is neither rare nor dominant in this matchup, indicating England faces real resistance rather than a routine victory. The full spread—England 63%, Draw 25%, Panama 12%—implies a match where the favorite is favored but not overwhelmingly so, and a stalemate ranks as a plausible outcome.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both lean notably toward England compared to the model. The market places England at 73–76%, roughly 10 percentage points higher than the model's 63%, while compressing the draw to 15–17% and Panama's win to 10% or lower. These are meaningful divergences, particularly in the England win probability. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is more accurate; users should weigh both signals when forming their own view.
